title: Bridgeway Connects
description: Answer a series of questions to help identify services and support that may be helpful. Your answers are not stored.

steps.title: Your Safety
steps.intro: We want to make sure you're safe. These first few questions help us understand your situation and connect you with appropriate support.

questions.label: Are you in immediate danger right now?

options.label: No, I am not in immediate danger

questions.helperText: If you are in immediate danger, consider calling 911 or a local emergency service if it is safe to do so.

questions.label: What is your preferred language?
What state are you in (or where do you need help)?
What best describes your current situation?

options.label: I'm unable to work due to health/disability

questions.label: Do you have a safe place to stay tonight?

options.label: Yes, I have a safe place
No, I do not have a safe place
I'm not sure if it's safe

questions.helperText: If you do not have a safe place to stay, the results page can highlight shelter and crisis resources.

steps.title: Your Work & Wages
steps.intro: This section asks about your work and how you are paid. These details help us understand your situation and what resources might help.

questions.label: What type of work do you do (or did you do)?
How long have you been (or were you) in this job?
Is this work legal in the US?

options.label: No, I don't think it's legal

questions.label: How are you typically paid?
What is the typical amount you receive per pay period?
questions.helperText: If you're unsure, give your best estimate. This helps us understand your situation.

questions.label: How often do you get paid?
How many hours do you typically work per day?
How many days per week do you work?
In a typical month, do you work every week, or are there weeks with no work?

options.label: I work every week of the month

questions.label: Besides your main pay, do you receive any additional compensation?
Are any deductions taken from your pay?
What deductions are taken from your pay?
Do you receive a pay stub or written record of what you earn?
Do you understand exactly how your pay is calculated?
Have you ever experienced any of these payment issues?
questions.helperText: It is okay if this hasn't happened to you. Answering honestly helps us understand your situation.

questions.label: If you asked about your pay or tried to resolve a payment problem, what happened?

options.label: I was able to resolve it
I was told it was a mistake and got corrected
I was threatened, yelled at, or retaliated against

questions.helperText: Threats can be direct or indirect. Your experience matters.

steps.title: Your Work Conditions & Safety
steps.intro: This section asks about what your workplace is like and whether you feel safe there.

questions.label: Are you able to take breaks during your work day?
How many breaks do you typically get per day?
Are you ever required to work overtime or extra hours?
If overtime is required, are you paid extra for those hours?
How many days off do you typically get per week?
Do you feel safe in your workplace?
If no or somewhat, what makes you feel unsafe?
Do you have access to necessary protective equipment?
Has anyone at work ever injured you or threatened to?

steps.title: Your Freedom & Control
steps.intro: This section asks about your freedom to make decisions and move around. These are important indicators of your situation.

questions.label: Are you free to leave your job whenever you want?
If you wanted to leave this job, what would happen?

options.label: I could leave freely, no consequences
I could leave but would lose pay/housing
I could leave but I'd face retaliation
My employer has threatened me if I try to leave
I don't think I'm allowed to leave

questions.helperText: If leaving feels unsafe, impossible, or controlled by someone else, that matters.

questions.label: Are you free to move around during non-work hours?
Is there anything preventing you from leaving your current situation right now?

options.label: Threats to my safety or family
I owe a debt I'm told I must pay off
I don't know where to go

questions.label: Do you have control of your own important documents (passport, ID, work permit, etc.)?
questions.helperText: This can include a passport, ID, work papers, money, or other personal documents.

questions.label: If no or partially, who has your documents and why?

options.label: I gave them as collateral for a loan
Someone else has them for another reason
I'm not sure where they are
I don't have documents to begin with

questions.label: Are you able to access your documents whenever you want?
Who makes decisions about your work schedule, time off, and work assignments?

options.label: I discuss it with my employer
Decisions are made by someone else (not my employer)

questions.label: Can you refuse a work assignment or activity you don't want to do?
Are you required to ask permission before doing normal things like:

options.label: Leaving work early or taking time off
Going to church or religious services

questions.label: Are you allowed to contact family, friends, or anyone outside of work?
Does your employer monitor or control your phone, email, or communications?
Do you have your own phone or access to one when you need it?
If you needed help right now, who could you call or reach out to?

options.label: I'm not sure who to call
I have no one to call

questions.label: Have you ever been threatened by your employer or anyone at work?
What kinds of threats have been made?

options.label: Threats of physical harm to me
Threats of harm to my family
Threats to take away housing or basic needs

questions.helperText: Threats can be direct or indirect, including threats against loved ones.

questions.label: Are you afraid of your employer?

steps.title: Sexual Experiences & Relationships
steps.intro: This section asks sensitive questions about sexual experiences. You do NOT have to answer. Your safety and privacy are our priority. Nothing you share will be judged. If you feel uncomfortable, you can skip these questions and still get support.

questions.label: Have you ever been asked or pressured to engage in sexual activity with someone for money, drugs, a place to stay, or something else?
If yes, tell us more about that situation. Who asked you?

options.label: A pimp, gang member, or trafficker
A friend or someone I knew
A police officer or authority figure
I'm not sure who was in control

questions.label: Were you able to refuse or say no?
If you couldn't refuse, why? What happened if you tried to say no?

options.label: I was threatened (with violence, deportation, arrest, etc.)
I was underage at the time
I was under the influence of drugs/alcohol
I felt trapped or had no other choice
Someone I depended on made me
Shame or guilt kept me quiet
I thought it was normal at the time

questions.label: How many times did this happen?
Over what time period did this happen?
How did you first get involved in sexual activity or "work"? How did you meet the person?

options.label: Through an app (dating, social media, job board)
Through a friend or someone I knew
A stranger approached me on the street
Through a job listing or advertisement
I was sold by family or someone

questions.label: How old were you when this first happened?
Were you offered anything to participate? (Payment, shelter, food, drugs, protection, etc.)
Was there anyone who benefited from this arrangement? (Besides the person using you)
If someone was profiting from you, did you know about it?
Who had control over where you lived?
Who had control over your money/income?
Did anyone take your identification documents, money, or other belongings?
Were you required to use a fake name, profile, or "stage name"?
Did you have to give any money you earned to someone else?
If you tried to stop or leave, what happened?

options.label: I was beaten or physically hurt
Someone threatened people I care about
I was told I'd be deported
I was denied food, money, or shelter
I was abandoned in an unfamiliar place
I was shamed or publicly humiliated
I was told I'd never see my children again
I was drugged or kept addicted
Nothing happened - they let me leave

questions.label: Were you using drugs or alcohol during this time?
Who provided the drugs or alcohol?

options.label: A partner or friend provided it
The person/people profiting from me provided it
It was given to me without my knowledge

questions.label: Did you feel like you needed drugs or alcohol to cope with the situation?
During this time, did you experience any health issues?
How would you describe your relationship with the person (or people) involved?
Is this situation still happening?

steps.title: Immigration & Legal Status
steps.intro: Your immigration status is not our concern. We're here to help, regardless of your legal status. This information helps us connect you with appropriate resources.

questions.label: What is your current immigration status?

options.label: Visa holder (work visa, student visa, etc.)

questions.helperText: Determines eligibility for certain visas (T visa, U visa, VAWA)

questions.label: Have you ever been in contact with immigration authorities?
Are you afraid of being deported?
Would you like information about immigration assistance?
questions.helperText: This does not determine eligibility. It only helps identify whether legal screening could be useful.

steps.title: What You Need & Next Steps
steps.intro: These final questions help us connect you with the resources and support that matter most to you.

questions.label: What do you need most right now?
questions.helperText: Select as many support needs as apply right now.

questions.label: Would you like us to connect you with services?
